posts.author: Xconchris
posts.content:
Ubermacht! Just the man i need to speak with....

Need confirmation that Jaeger means hunter in German

Also it means in the United States, a disgusting drink with a picture of a deer that I drank in college with red bull until 3am in the morning, vomiting in a trash can and made me late to advanced Spanish class at the University of Delaware 😎

* Edited at 03.20.2026,  7:56 AM ET *

posts.author: Ubermacht
posts.content: Hereby confirmed. Jäger=hunter. Where I'm from, mixing Jägermeister with Red Bull (I think you Anglos call it Jägerbomb) is considered heresy, though. Germans like to think we can hold our liquor, but Jägermeister has claimed many victims. So no worries, vomiting is just part of the authentic experience. I'm glad I haven't touched that stuff in 10 years.

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.content:
@Xconchris

I was pleasantly surprised to see Idris Elba get knighted this early as usually they wait until they're in their 70's or 80s to get them, sort of like a lifetime achievement award. The exception of course are athletes, especially Olympians as they seem to get knighthoods before their athletic careers are even over in some cases.

Jägerbombs are the shot of choice over here, if you're having a round of shots, it's usually a Jägerbomb with coke. Having said that, when I've been drinking with some younger colleagues (comparably younger, I'm 27 and my "young" colleagues are 19 - 22) their generation seem to go in for cherry Sambuca or cherry Tequila for some reason.

Also,

Nathaniel Wood
Mason Jones
Shem Rock
